title: NOC 72023 – Supervisors, railway transport operations | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Railway Transport Operations Supervisors (NOC 72023) are the strategic commanders of Canada's essential rail networks. As Canada expands its "First Transcontinental Railroad" connections with the US and Mexico and moves toward Digital Automatic Coupling (DAC), these supervisors are critical to ensuring the safe, punctual, and high-efficiency movement of freight and passengers. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they manage complex rail yard logistics and mainline operations, directly overseeing locomotive engineers and yard crews in an environment that is rapidly integrating AI-driven scheduling and real-time digital integrity monitoring.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Operational Command: Supervise, coordinate, and schedule the activities of railway and yard locomotive engineers, yard workers, and railway labourers.
Schedule Optimization: Establishing methods to meet strict work schedules and coordinating activities with other departments (maintenance, logistics, and safety).
Technical Problem Solving: Resolving complex operational problems and recommending measures to improve transit performance and fuel efficiency.
Safety & Training: Training staff in 2026 Canadian Rail Operating Rules (CROR), safety procedures, and new digital train-integrity monitoring technologies.
Reporting: Preparing detailed work reports and recommending personnel actions such as hiring, promotions, and specialized training.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Experience: Several years of experience in railway operations (as a locomotive engineer or yard worker) are mandatory.
Certification: A Canadian Rail Operating Rules (CROR) certificate is mandatory.
Professional Standards: A certificate in accordance with the Railway Employee Qualification Standards Regulations is required.
Regulatory Status: This occupation is not regulated in Canada, but internal corporate certification and government safety compliance are strictly enforced.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, driven by a 3.5% CAGR in the rail freight market and a wave of retirements in senior operations:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. On March 18, 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations specifically for NOC 72023 under the Employer Job Offer: REDI (Regional Economic Development through Immigration) pilot. Minimum scores for this draw started as low as 47, highlighting an urgent need for railway leadership in Ontario's industrial and transit hubs.

Alberta & Saskatchewan: Strong Demand. As the primary corridor for grain, potash, and energy exports, the Prairies offer the highest median wages for this NOC, with top-tier earners reaching $52.88/hr.
British Columbia: Good prospects. Demand is high in the "Pacific Gateway" for supervisors managing the critical link between the Port of Vancouver and the national rail network.
National Trend: Employment is extremely stable, with 100% of workers in this NOC receiving comprehensive non-wage benefits (pensions, dental, and medical).
Why Work in Canada as a Railway Supervisor?
Command High-Tech Infrastructure: Lead the implementation of Positive Train Control (PTC) and 2026-grade automated yard management systems.
Strategic Economic Role: Manage the backbone of North American trade, specifically handling the surge in "nearshoring" manufacturing traffic from Mexico to the Eastern U.S. and Canada.
Elite Compensation & Benefits: As a TEER 2 professional, your earning potential is high, with a national median wage of $40.73/hr and full job security within major rail companies like CPKC and CN.
Priority for PR: In March 2026, this NOC was specifically targeted in OINP draws, and it remains a core occupation for the 2026 Express Entry "Transport" category.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On March 18, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72023 with a score of 47+ under the REDI Pilot. This is a high-priority route for those with a job offer in Ontario.

2026 Express Entry (Transport Category): IRCC has prioritized "Transport Occupations" for category-based draws in 2026, allowing supervisors with 1 year of experience to receive invitations with lower CRS scores.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly launched federal initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Railway supervisors are classified as "High-Priority Essential Infrastructure Workers."
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for supervisors with a job offer from a BC-based railway or intermodal logistics firm.
